data.description:
## Context

The 2026-07-08 principal-architect audit (security / reliability / maintainability / OSS best practices; four deep-dive reviews plus bandit, semgrep, pip-audit, gitleaks, zizmor, radon) found no critical security issues but a cluster of high-leverage reliability seams and hygiene gaps. Reconciler-internal findings were routed to epic adept-hedge-stain (including the dead-safety-machinery story nonmystic-archetypic-gibbon); everything else lands here. Each child cites the audit finding it remediates.

## Success Criteria

- The store's cross-clone claim race is surfaced to the losing claimant and pinned by a two-clone test
- No git-store write path can commit another write's leftovers or race the push-retry merge
- The LLM gates fail closed on unreadable config and honor a wired timeout no lower than the SDK default
- Inbound Jira statuses that have no mapping can no longer silently reopen local tickets
- Typing and config-surface ratchets are in place (mypy strictness ratchet started; env-var registry with a CI drift gate)
- Docs drift called out by the audit is fixed; CodeQL runs on the mirror; docs are navigable by humans; repo hygiene items closed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child stories/tasks closed with green CI (Gerrit Verified +1 per change)
- [ ] Reliability children (claim-race surfacing, push-merge lock, staged-phantom reset, LLM timeout wiring, unmapped-status guard) landed with regression tests
- [ ] Maintainability children (mypy ratchet start, env-var registry + drift gate) landed
- [ ] Docs children (stale shell-test citations, stale architecture remedy row, api-stability version, docs index + user guide) landed
- [ ] OSS/CI children (CodeQL workflow, README badges, Python 3.13 matrix, .joe-janitor/.tokensave hygiene) landed
data.title: 2026-07-08 principal-architect audit remediation: reliability seams, gate hardening, maintainability ratchets, OSS hygiene
